hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in Marketing: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with a Marketing specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Bengaluru-based deemed university with 17+ specialisations. Consumer Behaviour, Integrated Marketing Communications, Omni Channel Marketing, B2B Marketing, and Retail Marketing in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA Marketing: NIRF #62, NAAC A++, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options: semester/yearly/lump-sum), 4 scholarship categories. Bengaluru-based deemed university with Python for Data Science in Sem 2 core. Strong for brand management, retail marketing, and integrated communications careers.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Marketing is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), a Bengaluru-based deemed university holding NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 in the University category, and NIRF #73 in Management (2025). The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Marketing specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Consumer Behaviour and Insights, Integrated Marketing Communications, Omni Channel Marketing, Market Research Tools, B2B Marketing, Retail Marketing and Service Management, Digital Marketing and Brand Management, and Global Marketing.

A distinctive feature across all JAIN MBA specialisations is Python for Data Science embedded in the Sem 2 core curriculum. Marketing students develop data literacy before entering Sem 3 marketing subjects, positioning them for analytics-enabled brand and campaign management roles. JAIN's Bengaluru base provides placement connect with South India's FMCG, IT services marketing, and e-commerce sectors.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Design quantitative and qualitative research studies to generate marketing insights and inform brand strategy decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply process design, quality management, and capacity planning to marketing operations and campaign execution.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behavior and Insights
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse buying behaviour models, psychographic segmentation, and cultural influences for marketing strategy development.

sem3Subjects.name: Integrated Marketing Communications
sem3Subjects.description: Design unified campaign briefs across ATL, BTL, digital, and PR channels with consistent brand messaging.

sem3Subjects.name: Omni Channel Marketing
sem3Subjects.description: Build seamless customer journeys across physical and digital touchpoints for retail and e-commerce brand management.

sem3Subjects.name: Market Research Tools
sem3Subjects.description: Apply SPSS, survey design, focus group methodology, and secondary data analysis to marketing research projects.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop competitive positioning, market entry strategy, and portfolio management for marketing-led business growth.

sem4Subjects.name: B2B Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Design account-based marketing strategies, manage complex sales cycles, and build B2B brand positioning.

sem4Subjects.name: Retail Marketing and Service Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage category strategy, visual merchandising, omnichannel retail execution, and service quality frameworks.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing and Brand Management
sem4Subjects.description: Execute SEO, paid media, and social campaigns; build brand equity frameworks for digital-first markets.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Marketing
sem4Subjects.description: Adapt brand strategy, pricing, and distribution for international market entry across emerging and developed markets.

sem4Subjects.name: Master Thesis / Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied marketing research project on brand strategy, consumer insights, or channel optimisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Marketing MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FMCG, E-commerce and retail, IT services marketing, Advertising and media agencies, BFSI marketing, EdTech

**Entry-level roles:** Brand Executive, Marketing Research Analyst, Digital Marketing Executive, Channel Sales Executive, Content Strategist

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Marketing Manager, Retail Category Manager, Digital Brand Manager, Trade Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, Head of Brand, Chief Marketing Officer, Marketing Director

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, Nestle, ITC, P&G, Amazon India, Flipkart, Myntra, Swiggy, Zomato, Infosys Marketing, Wipro Brand, HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential, Dentsu India, Publicis Sapient

skills.body:
The JAIN Marketing specialisation builds skills that FMCG and retail employers test at brand manager and marketing manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Consumer Behaviour and Insights: psychographic segmentation, attitude formation analysis, and buying behaviour framework application for brand insights and planning roles
- Integrated Marketing Communications: campaign brief writing, media mix planning, and cross-channel message consistency for marketing communications roles
- Omni Channel Marketing: customer journey mapping, digital-physical touchpoint design, and retail channel integration for e-commerce and retail management
- Market Research Tools: SPSS survey analysis, focus group design, and secondary market research for research and planning functions

**Applied skills:**
- B2B Marketing: account-based marketing, complex sales cycle management, and B2B brand positioning for enterprise marketing roles
- Digital Marketing and Brand Management: SEO execution, paid media management, and brand equity measurement for digital brand management

**Soft skills:** Presenting brand strategy to senior management, managing advertising agency relationships, negotiating trade marketing budgets with retailers, and leading cross-functional new product launches.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA Marketing against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Marketing:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Marketing has strong all-India brand recognition in Delhi-NCR FMCG and services. JAIN Marketing at Rs 1,75,000-1,96,000 adds Python for Data Science in the Sem 2 core, giving marketing students data skills Amity's standard curriculum does not include. For South India marketing careers, JAIN carries stronger Bengaluru ecosystem access.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Marketing Management:** NMIMS is NAAC A+ at Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000 with strong Mumbai brand recognition in FMCG and advertising. NMIMS does not offer a dedicated digital marketing specialisation. JAIN Marketing includes dedicated Omni Channel Marketing and Global Marketing as distinct Sem 4 subjects. For Bengaluru-based marketing careers, JAIN is competitive.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Marketing:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000 with Coursera Plus certifications. MUJ Marketing adds global Google and Meta certification pathways. JAIN Marketing includes dedicated B2B Marketing as a standalone subject. Both are comparable at similar fee levels.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Marketing is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. The Marketing specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA Marketing alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Pooja Shetty
items.liked: Omni Channel Marketing and Digital Marketing subjects were directly applicable to my e-commerce brand role in Bengaluru. Python for Data Science in Sem 2 gave me analytics skills my peers from other online MBAs lacked.
items.disliked: B2B Marketing could include more SaaS and tech sector examples relevant to Bengaluru companies.
items.body: I had 3 years in retail marketing and the JAIN MBA helped me move to brand manager. The Bengaluru brand recognition was strong at local FMCG and e-commerce companies. Fee range is reasonable with scholarship options.

items.name: Kiran Reddy
items.liked: Consumer Behaviour and Integrated Marketing Communications were well-structured. Global Marketing gave me frameworks for my export brand role.
items.disliked: Fee tier structure was initially confusing - needed counsellor clarification to understand which tier the Marketing spec falls under.
items.body: Good programme for South India marketing careers. Hyderabad FMCG and tech companies recognised the JAIN credential. Python in core was a genuine differentiator in interviews.

items.name: Sahana Kumar
items.liked: Retail Marketing and Service Management was directly applicable to my retail career.
items.disliked: LMS platform experience was below tier-1 university standards. Live session scheduling was not always convenient for working professionals.
items.body: Decent programme for the fee. Bengaluru brand is strong locally. For pan-India FMCG careers, Amity or NMIMS would carry stronger national recognition. For South India careers on a budget, JAIN works well.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the Marketing specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor as tier assignment can change across admission cycles.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Does JAIN offer dual specialization in Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. JAIN offers 7+ dual specialisation options including Finance and Marketing, Marketing and HRM, Marketing and Business Analytics, Finance and Business Analytics, HR and Business Analytics, HRM and Finance, and Digital Marketing and E-Commerce. Dual specs cover subjects from both domains in Sem 3-4, a differentiator not available at Amity, NMIMS, or MUJ at comparable fees.

faqs.question: Is Python for Data Science in JAIN core curriculum?
faqs.answer: Yes. Python for Data Science is a Sem 2 core subject for all JAIN MBA students regardless of specialisation. This means Marketing students develop foundational data analytics skills before entering Sem 3 marketing subjects - enabling data-driven brand management and marketing analytics roles.

faqs.question: Is JAIN Marketing MBA suitable for digital marketing careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital Marketing and Brand Management is a dedicated Sem 4 subject. Combined with Omni Channel Marketing and Market Research Tools, the programme builds digital brand management skills. Students specifically focused on performance marketing and SEO may also consider JAIN's Digital Marketing and E-Commerce dual specialisation.

chooseIf: You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NAAC A++ deemed university with Python for Data Science in the core curriculum.
You are targeting marketing careers in South India - Bengaluru, Hyderabad, or Chennai - where JAIN brand recognition is strong.
You want flexibility across 3 payment options (semester/yearly/lump-sum) and 4 scholarship categories.
consider: If pan-India FMCG or Mumbai marketing brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Marketing (Rs 2.25L) or NMIMS Marketing Management carry stronger national recognition.
If Coursera Plus global marketing certifications matter, MUJ Online MBA Marketing with Coursera Plus is a strong alternative.
If digital marketing is the primary focus, JAIN's Digital Marketing and E-Commerce dual specialisation may be more targeted.
